input.headline: Tell us: have you ever received a terrible Secret Santa?

raw.headline: Tell us: have you ever received a terrible Secret Santa?

winner: I got a whoopee cushion for Secret Santa once. It was a gift I never opened, out of sheer self-preservation.

candidates.text: My Secret Santa gave me a gift card for a store that doesn’t exist yet, with an expiration date set to next Tuesday—after they’re supposed to open.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 9.0, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist with a clear setup and punchline, concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: My Secret Santa gave me a gift—my name written on a napkin. I was too polite to ask if this was a *taste* of things to come.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with clever wordplay on 'taste' and politeness, concise and specific joke"}

candidates.text: I got my Secret Santa gift—a single sock with a note saying "You're welcome." I don't even own one sock, let alone a pair!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific scenario with a clever twist on the expectation of Secret Santa gifts."}

candidates.text: I got a whoopee cushion for Secret Santa once. It was a gift I never opened, out of sheer self-preservation.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is specific and has a surprising twist with the 'never opened' detail, but it's slightly generic and could be more concise or original."}

candidates.text: Me? Never received a terrible Secret Santa—my gift was so bad that everyone *wanted* it!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the twist is a bit weak and relies on a double meaning that may not land as effectively."}
